meta_title: For Founders: Antigravity project template to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Antigravity Project Template for Portfolios
content.seo_description: Overcome the no-portfolio barrier. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to ship high-converting SaaS MVPs in record time.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends when you stop building toy projects and start shipping production-ready SaaS modules. By leveraging the Antigravity project template paired with Claude Code, you can architect, scaffold, and deploy a high-fidelity MVP in hours, giving you a professional asset that demonstrates technical authority and business-ready execution.
content.intro:
The silence of a blank portfolio is deafening. You’ve got the vision and the technical ambition, but the market only respects one currency: shipped code. When you lack a track record, you don't need more tutorials; you need an architectural framework that bridges the gap between 'idea' and 'revenue-generating application'.

Enter the Antigravity project template. It is not just boilerplate code; it is a battle-tested foundation designed for rapid iteration and commercial viability. By combining this structure with the brute force of Claude Code, you transform from a beginner struggling with setup into a founder who builds at the speed of thought.

steps.title: Define the Business Logic
steps.description: Before writing a single line of code, clarify the SaaS value proposition. Use the template to structure your core feature set.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a SaaS project template focused on [Insert Niche] that includes user authentication, Stripe payment integration, and a clean dashboard layout.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize a new Antigravity project structure for a [Niche] SaaS, ensuring all standard middleware is configured.'

steps.title: Configure the Database Schema
steps.description: Define data relationships that scale. Focus on user-to-resource mapping to ensure your MVP handles multi-tenancy correctly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema for a SaaS platform with users, subscriptions, and project entities using Prisma or Drizzle.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate the schema.prisma file for a multi-tenant SaaS application and run the initial migration.'

steps.title: Implement Authentication and Billing
steps.description: Never build auth from scratch. Integrate industry-standard providers to handle the heavy lifting of user management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate NextAuth and Stripe checkout sessions into the existing Antigravity project structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Add authentication middleware and a Stripe checkout endpoint to the current project.'

steps.title: Build the Core Feature Module
steps.description: Construct the 'magic' of your application. This is the feature that solves your user's primary pain point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a functional API route and UI component for [Specific Feature] that interacts with our defined database models.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a new feature module for [Feature] including the API handler and a responsive frontend component.'

steps.title: Deployment and Verification
steps.description: Ship your project to a production environment to prove it works in the real world.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment configuration for Vercel or Railway for this Antigravity project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Verify project health, run tests, and output a deployment-ready configuration.'

pro_tips: Always build for multi-tenancy from Day 1; retrofitting user-isolation into a SaaS after launch is a developer nightmare.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests as you build; a tested MVP is 10x more impressive to potential clients than a buggy prototype.
Prioritize 'Time-to-First-Dollar' over feature completeness; if the payment flow works, the app is ready for beta testers.
Maintain a 'Changelog' file in your repo; it acts as a public record of your progress and demonstrates consistency to future partners.
Never hardcode environment variables; use a robust validation library like T3 Env to ensure your production environment never crashes due to missing keys.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, document the build process in a blog post, share the repo on GitHub, and start cold-emailing potential users to get your first five signups.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting the 'no portfolio' excuse hold your career back. You have the tools, the template, and the roadmap to ship a professional-grade SaaS today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
